questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A ball of mass 10 kg moving with a velocity 103 m s-1 along the x -axis, hits another ball of mass 20 kg which is at rest. After the collision, first ball comes to rest while the second ball disintegrates into two equal pieces. One piece starts moving along y -axis with a speed of 10 m s-1. The second piece starts moving at an angle of 30° with respect to the x -axis. The velocity of the ball moving at 30° with x -axis is x m s-1. The configuration of pieces after the collision is shown in the figure below. The value of x to the nearest integer is
questions.solution:
Let the velocity of 2nd  fragment is v→ then by conservation of linear momentum
10(103)i^=(10)(10j^)+10v→
⇒v→=103i^-10j^
|v→|=300+100=400=20 m s-1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two blocks (m=0.5 kg and M=4.5 kg) are arranged on a horizontal frictionless table as shown in the figure. The coefficient of static friction between the two blocks is 37. Then the maximum horizontal force that can be applied on the larger block so that the blocks move together is N. (Round off to the Nearest Integer) [Take g as 9.8 m s-2]
questions.solution:
amax=μg=37×9.8

F=(M+m)amax=5amax

=21 N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Four forces are acting at a point P in equilibrium as shown in figure. The ratio of force F1 to F2 is 1:x where x=_____ .
questions.solution:
Here, θ=45°

Applying equilibrium conditions,

Taking components along x & y

Considering right side positive, ∑Fx=0

F1+1sin45°-2sin45°=0F1=2sin45°-1sin45°

F1=2-12=2-12=12

Taking upward positive,∑Fy=0

1cos45°+2cos45°-F2=0F2=1cos45°+2cos45°

F2=2+12=2+12=32

F1:F2=1:3

x=3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A nucleus disintegrates into two nuclear parts, in such a way that ratio of their nuclear sizes is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Their respective speed have a ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is_________
questions.solution:
Using the law of Momentum conservation

m1v1+m2(-v2)=0
⇒m1v1=m2v2
⇒m1m2=v2v1⇒4πρr3134πρr323=v2v1

It is given

 r1r2=1213
and r1r23=v2v1Therefore,12=v2v1
⇒v1v2=21

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
If one wants to remove all the mass of the earth to infinity in order to break it up completely. The amount of energy that needs to be supplied will be x5GM2R where x is ________.

(Round off to the Nearest Integer)

(M is the mass of earth, R is the radius of earth, G is the gravitational constant)
questions.solution:
Energy given =Uf-Ui

=0--35GM2R

=35GM2R

x=3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A system to 10 balls each of mass 2 kg are connected via massless and unstretchable string. The system is allowed to slip over the edge of a smooth table as shown in figure. Tension on the string between the 7th  and 8th  ball is _____  N when 6th  ball just leaves the table.
questions.solution:
Acceleration,a=Net pulling forceTotal mass

a=6mg10m=6g10=6 m s-2

Considering ball 8,9 & 10 as system, tension between ball 7 & 8 can be written as,

T7,8=3m×a

=3×2×6=36 N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Three students S1, S2 and S3 perform an experiment for determining the acceleration due to gravity (g) using a simple pendulum. They use different lengths of pendulum and record time for different number of oscillations. The observations are as shown in the table.

Student No. Length of pendulum
(cm)		 Number of oscillations
(n)		 Total time for
n oscillations		 Time
period
(s)
1. 64.0 8 128.0 16.0
2. 64.0 4 64.0 16.0
3. 20.0 4 36.0 9.0

(Least count of length =0.1 m, least count for time =0.1 s)

If E1, E2 and E3 are the percentage errors in g for students 1, 2 and 3, respectively, then the minimum percentage error is obtained by student no          .
questions.solution:
T=2πℓg⇒g=4π2ℓT2
Δgg=Δℓℓ+2∆TT

ΔT= leastcountoftime ΔT0 numberofoscillations (n) 
Δgg=Δℓℓ+2ΔT0nT

As Δℓ and ΔT0 are same for all observations so
Δgg is minimum for highest value of ℓ, n and T

⇒ Minimum percentage error in g is for student number 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The length of a metallic wire is increased by 20% and its area of cross-section is reduced by 4%. The percentage change in resistance of the metallic wire is ________.
questions.solution:
The given data is 

l'=1.2lA'=0.96A

Using the formula R=ρlA, the new resistance is 

R'=1.2ρl0.96A

⇒R'=1.25R

⇒ΔR=1.25R-R=0.25R

Hence, the percentage increase is 

=ΔRR×100=0.25RR×100=25%

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A particle is moving along the <math><mi>x</mi></math> -axis with its coordinate with time <math><mi>t</mi></math> given by <math><mi>x</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>t</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>.</mo></math> Another particle is moving along the <math><mi>y</mi></math> -axis with its coordinate as a function of time given by <math><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>.</mo></math> At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the speed of the second particle as measured in the frame of the first particle is given as <math><msqrt><mi>v</mi></msqrt><mo>.</mo></math> Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> is ___________.
questions.solution:
v1→=8-6ti^

v2→=-24t2j^

v21→=580

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A particle is moving with constant acceleration a. Following graph shows v2 versus x (displacement) plot. The acceleration of the particle is _________ m s-2.
questions.solution:
The equation of the straight line, y=mx+c, from the above graph we get the equation, v2=2x+20, now differentiate it with respect to x, 
⇒2vdvdx=2⇒a=22=1 m s-2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: From the top of a tower, a ball is thrown vertically upward which reaches the ground in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>. A second ball thrown vertically downward from the same position with the same speed reaches the ground in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>. A third ball released, from the rest from the same location, will reach the ground in _____ s.
questions.solution:
When it is thrown upward, -h=v×6-12g62

When it is thrown downward, -h=-v×1.5-12g1.52

Therefore, 5h=536+9 h=45 m

When the ball is dropped, h=12gt2⇒45=12×10×t2⇒t=3 s

Alternate method, t=t1t2=6×1.5=3 s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: For a train engine moving with speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>–</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>, the driver must apply brakes at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>500</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> before the station for the train to come to rest at the station. If the brakes were applied at half of this distance, the train engine would cross the station with speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo> </mo><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is ______. (Assuming same retardation is produced by brakes)
questions.solution:
Given that, the initial velocity u=20 m/s, the distance of the station S1=500 m, and the final velocity v=0.

The relation between the distance travelled S by the train with its initial velocity u and its final velocity v is given by

v2= u2-2aS........................(1)

where, a indicates the retardation of the train.

Substitute the values corresponding to the initial condition into equation (1) and solve to calculate the retardation of the train.

0=(20)2-2×a×500a=0.4 m/s2

For the second scenario, the initial velocity u=20 m/s and the distance travelled by the train S2=250 m.

Use these values along with the value of the retardation into equation (1) and solve to calculate the final velocity of the train.

v2=(20)2-2×0.4×250= 200v= 200..........................(2)

Comparing the value of the final velocity obtained in equation (2) with the given expression, we get x= 200.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A particle of mass 10 g moves in a straight line with retardation 2x, where x is the displacement in SI units. Its loss of kinetic energy for above displacement is 10x-nJ. The value of n will be ________.
questions.solution:
Acceleration can be written as, a=vdvdx.

Given: a=-2x(-ve sign is due to retardation)

Therefore,

vdvdx=-2x⇒vdv=-2xdx

Integrating both sides, we get

12vf2-vi2=-2x22=x2⇒12mvf2-vi2=mx2

⇒|ΔKE|=0.01x2=10x-2

Hence, n=2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A swimmer can swim with velocity of 12 km/h in still water. Water flowing in a river has velocity 6 km/h. The direction with respect to the direction of flow of river water he should swim in order to reach the point on the other bank just opposite to his starting point is ________°. 

(Round off to the Nearest Integer) (find the angle in degree)
questions.solution:
12sinθ=vr

sinθ=12

θ=30°

∴ α=120°

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A swimmer wants to cross a river from point A to point B. Line AB makes an angle of 30° with the flow of the river. The magnitude of the velocity of the swimmer is the same as that of the river. The angle θ with the line AB should be _______°, so that the swimmer reaches point B.
questions.solution: Both velocity vectors are of same magnitude therefore resultant would pass exactly midway through them θ=30°

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
If the initial velocity in horizontal direction of a projectile is unit vector i^ and the equation of trajectory is y=5x1-x. The y component vector of the initial velocity is _____  j^

(Take g=10 m s-2)
questions.solution:
Given here, initial velocity, ux=1

Equation of trajectory, y=5 x1-x=5x-5x2

Now, differentiating the above equation, 

dydt=5dxdt-10xdxdt

For initial y-component of velocity

uy=dydtx=0⇒51=5

u→y=5j^

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An object is projected in the air with initial velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>u</mi></math> at an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi></math>. The projectile motion is such that the horizontal range <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math>, is maximum. Another object is projected in the air with a horizontal range half of the range of first object. The initial velocity remains same in both the case. The value of the angle of projection, at which the second object is projected, will be _____  degree.
questions.solution:
The maximum range is given by, Rmax=u2sin245°g=u2 g.

For the range to be half of the maximum range,

Rmax2=u22 g=u2sin2θg

⇒sin2θ=12

⇒2θ=30°or 2θ=150°

⇒θ=15°,75°

Therefore, answer is 15 or 75

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>F</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mo>(</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="italic">k</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> acts at a point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="italic">i</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="italic">j</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. Then the magnitude of torque about the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="italic">j</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi mathvariant="italic">k</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo></msqrt><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>.</mo></math>The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is..........
questions.solution:
r→=4-1i^+3-2j^+-1-1k^

=3i^+j^-2k^

τ=r→×F→=i^jk^31-2123

=i^7-j^11+k^5=7i^-11j^+5k^

=49+121+25=195

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: One end of a straight uniform 1m long bar is pivoted on horizontal table. It is released from rest when it makes an angle 30o from the horizontal (see figure). Its angular speed when it hits the table is given as n rad s-1 , where n is an integer. The value of n is ____________
questions.solution:
according to energy conservation

mgl2sin⁡30°=12ml23ω2
Solving
ω2=15
ω=15

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Consider a badminton racket with length scales as shown in the figure.

If the mass of the linear and circular portions of the badminton racket are same (M) and the mass of the threads are negligible, the moment of inertia of the racket about an axis perpendicular to the handle and in the plane of the ring at, r2 distance from the end A of the handle will be ______Mr2.
questions.solution:
IA=Mr22+M7r−r22+M(6r)212+M3r−r22

=Mr22+M(169)r24+3Mr2+25Mr24=171Mr24+37Mr24=2084Mr2=52Mr2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Moment of Inertia (M.I.) of four bodies having same mass M and radius 2R are as follows
I1= M.I. of solid sphere about its diameter
I2= M.I. of solid cylinder about its axis
I3= M.I. of solid circular disc about its diameter
I4= M.I. of thin circular ring about its diameter
If 2I2+I3+I4=xI1 then the value of x will be _____ .
questions.solution:
∵ 2I2+I3+I4=xI1

Now

For solid sphere about its diameter I1=25MR2,

For solid cylinder about its axis I2=12MR2,

For circular disc about its diameter I3=14MR2

And for circular ring about its diameter I4=12MR2

⇒2MR22+MR24+MR22=x×25MR2

⇒234+12=x×25

⇒2=x×25

⇒x=5

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A solid cylinder is released from rest from the top of an inclined plane of inclination 30° and length 60 cm. If the cylinder rolls without slipping, its speed upon reaching the bottom of the inclined plane is ______ m s-1. (Given g=10 m s-2)
questions.solution:
Let speed of cylinder upon reaching the bottom of the inclined plane be v.

Here, gain in kinetic energy=loss in potential energy

So, 12Iω2+12mv2=mglsinθ

⇒12mr22ω2+12mv2=mglsin30°

For pure rolling, v=rω

⇒12mr22vr2+12mv2=mglsin30°

⇒14mv2+12mv2=mglsin30°⇒34mv2=mgl2⇒v=4gl6

Putting the values, we have

⇒v=4×10×0.66=2 m s-1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An engine is attached to a wagon through a shock absorber of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The system with a total mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>000</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is moving with a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>72</mn><mo> </mo><mi>km</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> when the brakes are applied to bring it to rest. In the process of the system being brought to rest, the spring of the shock absorber gets compressed by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>90</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of energy of the wagon is lost due to friction, the spring constant is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>5</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Work =ΔK.E.

Wtriction +Wspring =0-12mv2

-9010012mv2+Wspring =-12mv2

wspring =-10100×12mv2

-12kx2=-120mv2⇒k=40000×(20)210×(1)2=16×105

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: As shown in the figure, a particle of mass 10 kg is placed at a point A. When the particle is slightly displaced to its right, it starts moving and reaches the point B. The speed of the particle at B is x m s-1. (Take g=10 m s-2 The value of x to the nearest integer is
questions.solution:
Using work-energy theorem, Wg=ΔK.E.
(10)(g)(5)=12(10)v2-0
v=10 m s-1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The potential energy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>U</mi></mfenced></math> of a diatomic molecule is a function dependent on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> (interatomic distance) as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>U</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>10</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>β</mi><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>5</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></math> where, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> are positive constants. The equilibrium distance between two atoms will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>α</mi></mrow><mi>β</mi></mfrac></mfenced><mfrac><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi></mfrac></msup><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>=</mo></math>______ .
questions.solution:
For equilibrium

dUdr=0

-10αr11+5βr6=0

5βr6=10αr11

r5=2αβ

r5=2αβ

r=2αβ15a=1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A ball of mass 100 g is dropped from a height h=10 cm on a platform fixed at the top of a vertical spring (as shown in figure). The ball stays on the platform and the platform is depressed by a distance h2. The spring constant is _____ N m-1
(Use g=10 m s-2)
questions.solution:
Loss in the gravitational potential energy of the ball= gain in the potential energy by the spring

-∆PEgravitational=∆PEspring

mgh+h2=12kh22⇒mg3h2=kh28

⇒k=12mgh=12×0.1×100.1=120 N m-1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A block of mass 'm' (as shown in figure) moving with kinetic energy E compresses a spring through a distance 25 cm when, its speed is halved. The value of spring constant of used spring will be nE N m-1 for n=_____.
questions.solution:
Using work - energy theorem

Wnet=∆KE

Wnet =Kf-Ki

⇒-12Kx2=12mv22-12mv2⇒-12Kx2=E4-E

⇒12Kx2=3E4⇒K=3E2x2

⇒K=3E2×142=24E N m-1

Thus, the value of n=24.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A uniform disc with mass M=4 kg and radius R=10 cm is mounted on a fixed horizontal axle as shown in figure. A block with mass m=2 kg hangs from a massless cord that is wrapped around the rim of the disc. During the fall of the block, the cord does not slip and there is no friction at the axle. The tension in the cord is _____ N.

(Take g=10 ms-2)
questions.solution:
Let a be the acceleration of block and α be the angular acceleration of disc.

Now, for unwrapping without slipping, Rα=a.

Forces acting on block are shown in FDB.

Using Newton's second law

mg-T=ma   ...1

Torque about centre of disc is τ=Ia

TR=MR22α=4R22α=⇒T=2Rα   ...2

Putting the value of tension in equation 1, we get

mg-2Rα=ma⇒mg-2RaR=ma⇒mg-2a=ma⇒ma+2a=mg⇒a=mgm+2=2×102+2=5 m s-2

Now, α=50.1=50 rad s-2

Tension in the cord is T=2×0.1×50=10 N.
